summary: DHH praises Apple's new Safari browser built on KHTML but argues it's missing tabbed browsing, a feature he considers essential. He notes that tabs are so fundamental that any browser without them is 'close to useless.' He links to an online petition urging Apple to add tabs to Safari and encourages readers to sign it, hoping to reach a critical mass of signatures that Apple can't ignore.
key_points: Safari is a welcome addition to Apple's software suite but lacks tabbed browsing
Tabbed browsing is such a transformative feature that it makes non-tabbed browsers unusable
DHH is unable to switch from Chimera to Safari without tabs
He promotes a petition to pressure Apple into adding the feature
money_quotes: Once you get used to browse by tabbing, there's no turning back, which renders every non-tab browser (or even editor) close to useless.
key_insight: Tabbed browsing is a non-negotiable feature that separates usable browsers from unusable ones, and Apple needs community pressure to get Safari right.
